Container.Name: claude-code-router
Container.Overview: Claude Code Router (CCR) — translates Anthropic-API requests into OpenAI-compatible requests so Claude Code can drive any local model (llama-server, llama-swap, vLLM, Ollama, etc.) as if it were Claude. Web UI at port 3456 for live config and routing inspection. [br]Default config in this template points at a local llama-swap on the same Docker network. Edit /root/.claude-code-router/config.json to map scenarios (default, think, longContext, background) onto specific upstream models.
Container.PostArgs: -c "d=/root/.claude-code-router; mkdir -p $d; if [ ! -f $d/config.json ]; then wget -qO $d/config.json https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iknox/unraid-templates/main/config-examples/claude-code-router.config.json; echo '[init] seeded config.json'; fi; if [ ! -f $d/strip-cch.js ]; then wget -qO $d/strip-cch.js https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iknox/unraid-templates/main/config-examples/claude-code-router.strip-cch.js; echo '[init] seeded strip-cch.js'; fi; exec pm2-runtime start /app/ecosystem.config.cjs"
Container.Description: CCR sits between Claude Code and your local OpenAI-compatible inference server. Inbound /v1/messages (Anthropic) → outbound /v1/chat/completions (OpenAI), with bidirectional translation of tool_use, reasoning blocks, and streaming SSE.

Config.Name: API
Config.Description: HTTP API + Web UI port. Set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://UNRAID-IP:3456 in your Claude Code shell.

Config.Name: Config dir
Config.Description: Holds config.json and rotating logs. Edit config.json to add upstream providers and route scenarios.

Config.Name: HOST
Config.Description: Bind address inside the container. 0.0.0.0 to accept LAN traffic.

Config.Name: PORT
Config.Description: In-container listen port. Match the published port above.

Config.Name: APIKEY
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Token clients send as 'x-api-key' or 'Authorization: Bearer ...'. CCR refuses to bind 0.0.0.0 unless this is set, so leaving it blank means the proxy is unreachable from the LAN. Generate a 32+ char random hex (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`).
